config_entries.label: Name
config_entries.description: The name for the sensor. At least one of **id** and **name** must be specified.

config_entries.label: Icon
config_entries.description: Manually set the icon to use for the sensor in the frontend.

config_entries.label: Address
config_entries.description: start address of the first register in a range (can be decimal or hexadecimal).

config_entries.label: Device Class
config_entries.description: The device class for the sensor. See [https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/sensor/#device-class](https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/sensor/#device-class) for a list of available options. Set to `""` to remove the default device class of a sensor.

config_entries.label: Unit Of Measurement
config_entries.description: Manually set the unit of measurement the sensor should advertise its values with. This does not actually do any maths (conversion between units).

config_entries.label: ID
config_entries.description: Manually specify the ID for code generation. At least one of **id** and **name** must be specified.

config_entries.label: Accuracy Decimals
config_entries.description: Set the number of digits after the decimal point that data consumers should use. While this does not change the value sent across the API, Home Assistant's ESPHome integration will round the value accordingly before using or storing it. The number of digits will also be used to set the entity's default display precision. Thus, one can display fewer decimals, but cannot increase precision of sensor values in Home Assistant. (The default value varies depending on component and sensor.)

config_entries.label: Bitmask
config_entries.description: sometimes multiple values are packed in a single register's response. The bitmask can be used to extract a value from the response. See Bitmasks.

config_entries.label: Custom Command
config_entries.description: raw bytes for modbus command. This allows using non-standard commands. If custom_command is used address and register_type can't be used. Custom data must contain all required bytes including the modbus device address. The CRC is automatically calculated and appended to the command. See Using custom_command how to use custom_command.

config_entries.label: Disabled By Default
config_entries.description: If true, then this entity should not be added to any client's frontend, (usually Home Assistant) without the user manually enabling it (via the Home Assistant UI). Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Entity Category
config_entries.description: The category of the entity. See [https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/core/entity/#generic-properties](https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/core/entity/#generic-properties) for a list of available options. Set to `""` to remove the default entity category.

config_entries.label: Expire After
config_entries.description: Manually set the time in which the sensor values should be marked as “expired”/“unknown”. Not providing any value means no expiry.

config_entries.label: Filters
config_entries.description: Specify filters to use for some basic transforming of values. See [Sensor Filters](https://esphome.io/components/sensor#sensor-filters) for more information.

config_entries.label: Force New Range
config_entries.description: If possible sensors with sequential addresses are grouped together and requested in one range. Setting force_new_range: true enforces the start of a new range at that address.

config_entries.label: Force Update
config_entries.description: If true, this option will force the frontend (usually Home Assistant) to create a state changed event when the sensor updates even if the value stayed the same. Some applications like Grafana require this when working with Home Assistant, but beware it can significantly increase the database size. Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Internal
config_entries.description: Mark this component as internal. Internal components will not be exposed to the frontend (like Home Assistant). Only specifying an `id` without a `name` will implicitly set this to true.

config_entries.label: Lambda
config_entries.description: Lambda to be evaluated every update interval to get the new value of the sensor. Parameters passed into the lambda note: because the response contains data for all registers in the same range you have to use data[item->offset] to get the first response byte for your sensor. Possible return values for the lambda.

config_entries.label: Offset
config_entries.description: Offset from start address in bytes (only required for uncommon response encodings). If more than one register is written in a command this value is used to find the start of this datapoint relative to start address. The component calculates the size of the range based on offset and size of the value type. For coil or discrete_input registers offset is the position of the coil/register because these registers encode 8 coils in one byte.

config_entries.label: Register Count
config_entries.description: The number of consecutive registers this read request should span or skip in a single command. Default is 1. See Optimizing modbus communications for more details.

config_entries.label: Register Type
config_entries.description: type of the modbus register.

config_entries.label: Response Size
config_entries.description: Size of the response for the register in bytes. Defaults to register_count*2.

config_entries.label: Skip Updates
config_entries.description: By default, all sensors of a modbus_controller are updated together. For data points that don't change very frequently, updates can be skipped. A value of 5 would only update this sensor range in every 6th update cycle. Note: The modbus_controller groups components by address ranges to reduce number of transactions. All components with the same starting address will be updated in one request. skip_updates applies for all components in the same range.

config_entries.label: State Class
config_entries.description: The state class for the sensor. See [https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/core/entity/sensor/#available-state-classes](https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/core/entity/sensor/#available-state-classes) for a list of available options. Set to `""` to remove the default state class of a sensor.

config_entries.label: Value Type
config_entries.description: data type of the modbus register data. The default data type for modbus is a 16 bit integer in big endian format (MSB first).
